published_prior_art.cite: schroder2025conformal: continuous-treatment causal conformal prediction uses the same propensity-ratio change-of-measure representation.
jin2023robustconformal: robust likelihood-ratio brackets are established, but no verified result gives this particular graded polynomial-tail integral modulus.
jin2023robustconformal: robust weighted conformal inference supplies finite-sample counterfactual coverage under likelihood-ratio bounds.
tibshirani2019covariateshift: weighted conformal prediction gives distribution-free covariate-shift coverage with a known likelihood ratio.
Kiyani, Pappas, and Hassani (2024): length optimization under generic covariate-shift validity is close but does not characterize a graded causal ratio-certificate tail frontier.
No verified paper gives a matched high-probability oracle-regret order jointly in n, delta, epsilon, gamma, and eta for certified causal ratio brackets.

novelty_flags.label: Proposition causal-change-of-measure
novelty_flags.one_line: The causal ratio change-of-measure is a baseline already used by schroder2025conformal, not a new kernel.

novelty_flags.label: Proposition robust-wcp-coverage
novelty_flags.one_line: jin2023robustconformal already supplies robust conformal coverage under likelihood-ratio bounds; this proposition may remain only as a baseline.

novelty_flags.label: Proposition known-ratio-reduction
novelty_flags.one_line: The known-ratio weighted-conformal reduction is covered by tibshirani2019covariateshift.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Questions tail-local-population-price and tail-local-sample-regret
novelty_flags.one_line: Add Kiyani, Pappas, and Hassani (NeurIPS 2024), which studies length-optimal conformal sets under covariate-shift validity; it does not subsume the proposed tail-certificate frontier but is a closer omitted comparator.

soundness_flags.label: def:robust-wcp
soundness_flags.one_line: For unrestricted measurable V_epsilon, the displayed minimum need not be attained and its conditional-normalization constraint is not a finite-dimensional optimization; specify a finite certificate representation or an explicit measurable envelope functional.

soundness_flags.label: def:robust-wcp and prop:robust-wcp-coverage
soundness_flags.one_line: r_rob is the worst-case quantile (and hence corresponds to a union/envelope of compatible intervals), while min_{v} p_v(y)>alpha forms their intersection; this extremum mismatch cannot license the stated simultaneous coverage or the claim that Ihat_n competes with I_rob.

soundness_flags.label: ass:compatible-target-law, prop:robust-wcp-coverage, and def:minimax-regret
soundness_flags.one_line: Only the marginal law of T is specified; coverage and regret require a joint target draw (T,Y^v) distributed under Q_v and independent of D_n, which is not stated.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Certificate-based coverage and both tail-local frontiers
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The bracket ell<=w<=u and its tail/width guarantees are externally certified inputs, not learned or validated from D_n.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All baseline coverage and any population or sample frontier are conditional on a valid externally certified bracket satisfying the stated tail and graded-width conditions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace def:robust-wcp by a computable worst-case score-CDF/quantile envelope whose extremum agrees with r_rob, and state the joint independent Q_v target draw before retaining any finite-sample coverage claim.
Add Kiyani, Pappas, and Hassani (2024) and explicitly distinguish its generic covariate-shift length optimization from a sharp heavy-tail, conditionally normalized ratio-bracket regret frontier.
Give the certificate a finite-support or finite-basis representation, or remove the finite-dimensional assertion and formulate a valid optimization/attainment condition for V_epsilon.
After the robust baseline is repaired, state a shell-richness lower condition and a candidate matched delta-plus-tail population bound before promoting either OEQ to a conjecture.

recommended_next_step: Revise the robust target-law and envelope construction, add the omitted length-optimization comparator, then retain the two sharp tail-frontier questions as the field-tier novelty kernel.
